C.description: Catchers need high awareness to manage the game and a strong arm to throw out base stealers.

1B.description: First basemen need quick reactions for ground balls and composure for handling throws.

2B.description: Second basemen need quick reactions, agility for double plays, and acrobatics for diving stops.

3B.description: Third basemen need elite reactions for hot corner plays and a strong arm for throws across the diamond.

SS.description: Shortstops are the most demanding defensive position, requiring elite reactions and a strong arm.

LF.description: Left fielders need good range (acrobatics/agility) and an adequate arm.

CF.description: Center fielders cover the most ground and need elite acrobatics and agility.

RF.description: Right fielders need a strong arm for throws to third base and good range.

P.description: Pitchers primarily focus on pitching; fielding is less emphasized.
